description: Bundled performance cards for airspeed terminology, takeoff/climb references, runway terminology and power setting discipline.

cards.front: What is VMC in the DHC-6 AFM?
cards.back: Minimum control speed single engine: the lowest speed at which the aircraft is controllable in flight in takeoff configuration with one engine at maximum power and the other feathered.

cards.front: At maximum takeoff weight, what is best rate of climb speed with 10° flap?
cards.back: 80 KIAS, regardless of number of engines operating.

cards.front: At maximum takeoff weight, what is best rate of climb speed with flaps up and both engines operating?
At maximum takeoff weight, what configuration is required for best rate of climb with one engine operating?
cards.back: 80 KIAS with 10° flap, functioning engine at 96% NP and maximum continuous power, the other propeller feathered, and flaps precisely set to 10°.
Maximum flap extended speed: the highest speed permitted with flaps in a specified position.
Gust penetration speed: the maximum speed recommended for flight in rough air.
Distance from a standing start with full takeoff power set before brake release to reach 50 ft AGL with both engines, or 35 ft AGL one-engine-inoperative.
Distance to accelerate from standing start to the specified decision/rotation speed, reject the takeoff, and stop completely.

cards.front: What is the difference between wet and contaminated runway in the AFM terminology?
cards.back: Wet runway has a shiny water layer without significant standing water; more than 0.125 in / 3 mm standing water is considered contaminated.

references.locator: Section 0.13, Wet Runway and Contaminated Runway

cards.front: Are reduced power takeoffs approved in the Revision 53 AFM baseline?
cards.back: No. Permission for reduced power takeoffs in Series 300 DHC-6 aircraft was rescinded; calculated takeoff torque must be set for each takeoff.

references.locator: Section 0.8.3, Withdrawal of Approval for Reduced Power Take-Offs
